Title: Switchable client specificity in a dual functional chaperone coordinates light-harvesting complex biogenesis

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Landsberg erecta-0 (Ler-0) background used in planta; recombinant protein from A. thaliana
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Ler-0; chaos mutant complemented with transgenic cpSRP43 variants
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chloroplast signal recognition particle 43-kDa protein; ATP-independent chaperone with substrate-binding domain (SBD) and C-terminal chromodomains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed from endogenous cpSRP43 promoter in transgenic Arabidopsis seedlings
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Rational mutagenesis shifting conformational equilibrium (R189L, DE3NQ, DE6NQ); 19F-NMR; circular dichroism; LHCP aggregation/solubilization assays; peptide binding anisotropy; thermoprotection assays for GUN4 and GluTR; Arabidopsis chaos complementation; heat-stress immunoblotting
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress/elevated temperature (especially 42°C); ionic strength (50-200 mM NaCl); low-dose urea; cycloheximide during heat-shock assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: cpSRP43 coordinates LHCP biogenesis with chlorophyll biosynthesis by switching client specificity: closed cpSRP43 promotes LHCP chaperoning, while open cpSRP43 protects chlorophyll-biosynthetic enzymes during heat stress
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cpSRP43 undergoes a reversible temperature-dependent switch between closed and open conformations. 2. The closed state specifically binds the FDPLGL motif in LHCP and prevents LHCP aggregation. 3. The open state thermoprotects chlorophyll biosynthesis enzymes including GUN4 and GluTR and stabilizes GUN4/CHLH in planta during heat stress. 4. R189L biases cpSRP43 open and enhances TBS-enzyme protection, whereas DE3NQ/DE6NQ stabilize the closed state and favor LHCP chaperoning.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to improve photosynthetic thermotolerance and maintain chlorophyll biosynthesis/LHC assembly balance under heat stress; conformational tuning may enhance stress resilience
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Open-biased state reduces LHCP chaperoning; closed-biased state weakens protection of tetrapyrrole biosynthesis enzymes during heat stress
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it is a dual-function chaperone, not a pathway enzyme or transcriptional regulator; major focus of the Results section.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Steady-state protein levels assessed in 18-day-old seedlings
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fluorescence anisotropy with 54C peptide; temperature-dependence analysis; immunoblotting in transgenic plants
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: cpSRP54 stabilizes the closed conformation of cpSRP43 and inhibits cpSRP43-mediated thermoprotection of tetrapyrrole-biosynthesis enzymes; it dissociates from cpSRP43 at elevated temperature
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cpSRP54 preferentially binds closed cpSRP43. 2. Its interaction with cpSRP43 weakens strongly with increasing temperature largely independently of cpSRP43 conformational equilibrium. 3. Release of cpSRP54 at high temperature contributes to switching cpSRP43 client preference from LHCPs to tetrapyrrole-biosynthesis enzymes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential modulator of cpSRP43 client switching for photosynthetic heat adaptation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Maintaining cpSRP54-cpSRP43 association may support LHCP biogenesis but suppress thermoprotection of chlorophyll-biosynthesis enzymes under heat stress
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because its direct role in modulating cpSRP43-dependent nutrient metabolism was experimentally tested in the Results.

Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as model tetrapyrrole biosynthesis (TBS) enzyme substrate for cpSRP43 thermoprotection; not itself shown here as the manipulated causal gene for metabolite change but is a core experimentally tested chlorophyll-pathway client
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Mg-protoporphyrin IX formation is facilitated indirectly via magnesium chelatase function
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for stability/function of a chlorophyll-biosynthetic step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GUN4 is heat-labile and rapidly aggregates in vitro and declines in planta during 42°C treatment. 2. Open-biased cpSRP43 (R189L) protects GUN4 more effectively than WT or closed-biased variants. 3. GUN4 steady-state levels are restored in cpSRP43-complemented lines under normal conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heat-induced aggregation kinetics; turbidity-based thermoprotection assays; in planta heat-shock immunoblotting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 42°C heat stress; varying NaCl concentration in vitro; cycloheximide pretreatment in planta
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Regulatory factor that stimulates/stabilizes magnesium chelatase during insertion of Mg2+ into protoporphyrin IX
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Heat-induced instability reduces chlorophyll precursor formation unless protected
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Stabilization of GUN4 or enhancement of its chaperoning could improve chlorophyll maintenance under heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heat stress destabilizes GUN4, and open cpSRP43 protects/stabilizes it, supporting chlorophyll biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Experimentally tested as a second tetrapyrrole-biosynthesis enzyme client of cpSRP43 in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GluTR is protected from heat-induced aggregation by cpSRP43. 2. Open-biased cpSRP43 provides strongest thermoprotection, whereas closed-biased cpSRP43 is less effective. 3. cpSRP43 chromodomains directly contribute to GluTR protection.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Thermoprotection turbidity assays with cpSRP43 full-length and SBD variants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Heat lability can compromise precursor supply for chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Protecting GluTR stability may enhance chlorophyll biosynthetic robustness under heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Open cpSRP43 thermoprotects GluTR, helping maintain chlorophyll precursor synthesis

Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GUN4; cpSRP43 (indirectly/protected as TBS client in planta)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein monitored during short-term heat stress in 10-day-old seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as an in planta model tetrapyrrole biosynthesis enzyme whose heat stability depends on cpSRP43 conformational state
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHLH protein decreases rapidly during short-term heat stress in plants expressing WT cpSRP43. 2. Open-biased cpSRP43 R189L stabilizes CHLH more effectively than WT or closed-biased variants during heat stress. 3. This supports enhanced protection of chlorophyll-biosynthetic machinery by open cpSRP43 in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In planta heat-shock assay with cycloheximide followed by immunoblotting
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Heat-induced loss of CHLH would impair chlorophyll synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Improving CHLH protection may support photosynthetic performance under transient heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Open cpSRP43 stabilizes CHLH abundance during heat stress, supporting chlorophyll biosynthesis
